title: Stacked Changes, Maintainer Workflows, and Integration Queues · Conflict Triage
summary: A responder inherits a plausible but defective stacked-change and maintainer integration protocol; preserve reachable history, isolate the first causal Git-state mismatch, and retain a regression probe. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in stacked-change and maintainer integration protocol and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Represent dependent changes as explicit base and tip relationships, keep each review independently testable, and update downstream stacks after revision.
Use range-diff and interdiff-style evidence to explain rerolls, reordered commits, changed commit messages, and semantic deltas between series.
Compare topic-branch, integration-branch, subsystem, patch-flow, merge-queue, and release-train workflows by authority and recovery cost.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-advanced cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Inspect the failure record
steps.instruction:
debug case — Case G85 is a debug in a disposable repository. the reroll changes only the requested validation patch and preserves stack dependencies. The learner must predict old and new series map by patch identity with reordered and amended entries visible, run git range-diff main...stack-v1 main...stack-v2, explain one patch changed, two map exactly, and the dependent tip moved to a new parent, test this changed condition: reorder the dependency after its consumer and reject the stack despite an equal final tree, and preserve this recovery boundary: retain both series refs and review comments until the integrated exact revision is published. Competency probe: Represent dependent changes as explicit base and tip relationships, keep each review independently testable, and update downstream stacks after revision.

steps.title: Implement the cause-level repair
steps.instruction: Append an Advanced Git evidence block headed "# Evidence: gita-collaboration-policy-defense-jbip6" with exact before and after refs; a falsifiable prediction; the bounded simulator command; expected, observed, tree, behavior, changed-case, and recovery evidence; and one honest real-repository transfer limit.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Gita Collaboration Policy Defense into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: gita-collaboration-policy-defense-jbip6
# scenario-key: trace-jbip6 probe-6pibj boundary-ip6jb owner-jbip6x fault-jbip6z
# before-ref: main=a11cejb candidate=b00b1p6
# hypothesis: the reroll changes only the requested validation patch and preserves stack dependencies
git range-diff main...stack-v1 main...stack-v2
# expected: old and new series map by patch identity with reordered and amended entries visible
# observation: one patch changed, two map exactly, and the dependent tip moved to a new parent
# changed-case: reorder the dependency after its consumer and reject the stack despite an equal final tree
# after-ref: main=a11cejb candidate=c0ffee6
# tree-proof: the affected tree and path set match the predicted repository transition
# behavior-proof: a changed input preserves the named product or release invariant
# recovery: retain both series refs and review comments until the integrated exact revision is published
# transfer-limit: provider merge queues need separate event, identity, permission, and tested-revision verification
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.
